scope_summary.problem: score issues CLI has list/show/sync (read path) but no write path (create/update/close) and no search. Agents cannot fully manage the issue lifecycle without shelling out to gh directly, breaking the uniform backend abstraction.
scope_summary.success: All IssueBackend verbs (create, update, close, search) implemented uniformly across Local + GitHub + GitLab backends. score issues CLI exposes all verbs with --json output. Cross-artifact references (related:/implements:) wired into frontmatter schema.
scope_summary.boundary: In scope: create, update, close, find verbs + IssueBackend trait extension + GitLab backend (glab shell-out) + cross-reference fields. Out of scope: Jira backend, bidirectional sync conflict resolution, authoring agent (#1179 second half).

questions.question: Should score issues create always create a local draft first, then optionally push? Or should there be a direct-to-GitHub mode?
questions.answer: Support both. Default: local-first (creates .score/issues/ draft with state: draft). Add --remote flag for direct-to-GitHub (calls gh issue create, writes local file with id backfilled). Agent flexibility: draft offline, push later; OR create directly when online.
questions.rationale: Agent may be working offline (no gh access) or online (wants immediate tracking). Supporting both gives maximum flexibility without complexity — the difference is just whether gh issue create runs before or after local file write.

questions.question: For score issues update, should body editing be in-place (rewrite full body from --body-file) or patch-based?
questions.answer: Full body replacement via --body-file. Agent generates complete content, passes it in. No partial patching.
questions.rationale: Agents already produce complete content. Patch-based editing adds complexity (section detection, merge logic) with no real benefit since the agent rewrites the whole body anyway.

questions.question: Should related:/implements: references be validated at write-time or list-time?
questions.answer: Warn at list-time only. Write accepts any reference without validation.
questions.rationale: Write-time validation creates ordering dependencies — agent can't create an issue referencing a change that doesn't exist yet. List-time warnings let agents work in any order, then check consistency afterward.

questions.question: Is there a GitLab repo available for integration testing?
questions.answer: Unit tests only with mocked glab CLI output. No GitLab repo available for integration testing.
questions.rationale: Real GitLab integration testing requires a running GitLab instance. Mock the glab binary's JSON output to verify parsing and error handling. Add integration tests later when a GitLab repo is available.
